---
title: "Sample Blog Post — Delete This"
description: "This post shows the patterns available for your writing."
author: "Pranay Kotasthane"
date: "2025-01-15"
categories:
  - Semiconductor Geopolitics
draft: true
---

<!-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OPTIONAL: If this post originally appeared in a newspaper/magazine,
  add a source note in the left margin like this:
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

::: {.source-note .column-margin}
**Mint**
Originally published as part of [The Intersection](#) column, every other Monday.
:::

Your first paragraph goes here. Write normally in Obsidian, convert to QMD, and drop the file in this folder.

<!-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  OPTIONAL: Pull quotes appear in the right margin automatically.
  Use this pattern anywhere in the article:
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

::: {.pullquote .column-margin}
A sentence worth pulling out of the text and highlighting for the reader.
:::

Continue writing your article here. The pull quote will appear beside this paragraph in the right margin on wide screens.

## A section heading

More content. Section headings stay in sans-serif to contrast with the serif body.

> A blockquote looks like this — amber left border, slightly indented.
